title: Redcliffe Dolphins

infobox.value: Redcliffe District Rugby League Football Club Inc.
[Hostplus Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"), [Brisbane Rugby League](/wiki/Brisbane_Rugby_League "Brisbane Rugby League")

are a semi-professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") club based in [Redcliffe](/wiki/Redcliffe,_Queensland "Redcliffe, Queensland"), [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). Founded in 1947, they were accepted into the [Brisbane Rugby League (BRL) premiership competition](/wiki/Brisbane_Rugby_League_premiership_(rugby_league_competition) "Brisbane Rugby League premiership (rugby league competition)") in 1960, and since 1996 have played in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup").

["Dolphins granted licence to become NRL's 17th team in 2023"](https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/10/13/dolphins-to-become-nrls-17th-team-in-2023/). *National Rugby League*. 12 October 2021. Retrieved 13 Octob

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian rugby league club

This article is about the rugby league club that currently plays in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"). For the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") team, see [Dolphins (NRL)](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)").

Redcliffe Dolphins

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |

| Runners-up (2nd grade) | **6** (1999, 2001, 2007, 2012, 2016, 2022) |
| Premierships (3rd grade) | **8** (1997, 2003, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2014, 2016, 2025) |

**Redcliffe Dolphins** are a semi-professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") club based in [Redcliffe](/wiki/Redcliffe,_Queensland "Redcliffe, Queensland"), [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland"), [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia"). Founded in 1947, they were accepted into the [Brisbane Rugby League (BRL) premiership competition](/wiki/Brisbane_Rugby_League_premiership_(rugby_league_competition) "Brisbane Rugby League premiership (rugby league competition)") in 1960, and since 1996 have played in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup").

A separately licensed team, simply called the [*Dolphins*](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)"), competes in the fully professional [National Rugby League (NRL)](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) while the Redcliffe Dolphins squad continues to play in the Queensland competition.

The BRL reached its competitive and attendance peak in the 1980s and then began to decline in favour of an expanded [NSWRFL Winfield Cup](/wiki/Winfield_Cup "Winfield Cup") competition. In 1988, the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos"), featuring many BRL players, began competing in the NSWRL, overshadowing the BRL. A new Queensland Cup competition began in 1996, in which the Redcliffe Dolphins were inaugural runners-up.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1997, the Dolphins won both the final BRL competition and the Queensland Cup.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Through their NRL license, the Dolphins organisation has become the only former BRL club to regain top-flight status.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redcliffe_Dolphins&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Founded on 27 February 1947, Redcliffe District Rugby League Football Club Inc. (known as Shellgrit) entered Under 17, Reserve Grade and First Grade teams in that year's Sandgate Suburban Rugby League competition. Through the 1950s, Redcliffe also played in the Kilcoy, Murrumba and Geraghty Cup competitions. On 19 November 1959, Redcliffe received full district club status and was accepted into the Brisbane first grade competition.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Throughout the 1960s, a number of famous players came through the ranks of the Dolphins to represent Queensland and [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_rugby_league_team "Australia national rugby league team"), including Trevor Harken and [Arthur Beetson](/wiki/Arthur_Beetson "Arthur Beetson"). In 1965, Redcliffe won its first Brisbane Rugby League Premiership. In 1972, Redcliffe were coached by former Kangaroo [Ken Day](/wiki/Ken_Day_(rugby_league) "Ken Day (rugby league)"). Redcliffe's halfback [Greg Oliphant](/wiki/Greg_Oliphant "Greg Oliphant") was selected to go on the [1978 Kangaroo tour](/wiki/1978_Kangaroo_tour "1978 Kangaroo tour") but did not play in any [Test matches](/wiki/Test_match_(rugby_league) "Test match (rugby league)"). Australian national coach [Frank Stanton](/wiki/Frank_Stanton_(rugby_league) "Frank Stanton (rugby league)") coached Redcliffe in 1980. The Redcliffe club won further premierships in 1994, 1996 and 1997 and has also appeared in 12 [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") grand finals, making it the most successful team in that competition.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e9/Redcliffe_dolphins_logo.jpg/250px-Redcliffe_dolphins_logo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Redcliffe_dolphins_logo.jpg)

The logo of the Redcliffe Dolphins from the early 2000s (decade) to 2005

In 2004, a Gold Coast NRL team was proposed under the name of Gold Coast Dolphins. In response to the threat of legal action from Redcliffe the team entered the NRL in 2007 as the [Gold Coast Titans](/wiki/Gold_Coast_Titans "Gold Coast Titans").

Redcliffe defeated Toowoomba in the 2006 Queensland Cup grand final at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Suncorp_Stadium "Suncorp Stadium") under the coaching of [Anthony Griffin](/wiki/Anthony_Griffin_(rugby_league) "Anthony Griffin (rugby league)").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2018, Redcliffe won their sixth Queensland Cup title, defeating [Easts Tigers](/wiki/Brisbane_Tigers "Brisbane Tigers") 36-22.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4c/Redcliffe_player_tackled_1a.jpg/250px-Redcliffe_player_tackled_1a.jpg)](/wiki/File:Redcliffe_player_tackled_1a.jpg)

Redcliffe Dolphins tackled by Easts at [Langlands Park](/wiki/Langlands_Park "Langlands Park") in 2011.

## NRL Team

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redcliffe_Dolphins&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: NRL Team")]

Main article: [Dolphins (NRL)](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)")

From 2023 onwards, the Redcliffe Dolphins' fully professional spin-off team, known simply as the [Dolphins](/wiki/Dolphins_(NRL) "Dolphins (NRL)"), compete separately in the National Rugby League.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Notwithstanding, the semi-professional Redcliffe Dolphins continue to compete as they are in the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") and other Queensland rugby league competitions.

## Home ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redcliffe_Dolphins&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Home ground")]

Redcliffe's current home ground, [Kayo Stadium](/wiki/Moreton_Daily_Stadium "Moreton Daily Stadium"), formerly known as Moreton Daily Stadium and Dolphin Oval, was first opened in 1979. After multiple upgrades between 2016 and 2020, the stadium has an approximate capacity of 11,500 including 10,000 seats.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
